name: Jules Bledsoe
abstract: Julius Lorenzo Cobb Bledsoe (December 29, 1897 – July 14, 1943) was an American baritone, a leading figure in the Harlem Renaissance, the first major Black opera singer in the United States, and one of the first Black artists to gain regular employment on Broadway.

works.t: Scipione, HWV 20: Hear me ye winds and waves
Faust: Act IV. Vous qui faites l'enformie

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: Julius Bledsoe, a guy like that, you never heard of him I’ll bet. But he was before Paul Whiteman... He had the biggest names, vocalists in the world.
about_quotes.doc_title: Doc Cheatham interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 28, 1995
